message:
fix(patches): regenerate QuickJS patch with correct format

- Fixed corrupt patch at line 322 (malformed hunk header)
- Corrected hunk header: @@ -1291,6 +1295,40 @@ → @@ -1291,6 +1295,43 @@
- Removed trailing blank context line that caused git apply to fail
- Added scripts/apply-quickjs-patches.sh for CI and users
- Patch now applies cleanly with git apply

Issue: The old patch had a hunk header claiming 40 lines when there were 43.
This caused git apply to report "corrupt patch at line 322".

Solution: Manually applied all changes using BSD patch (6/8 hunks succeeded),
then manually fixed the 2 failed hunks, and regenerated the patch using
git diff --cached to ensure correct format.

The new patch:
- Applies cleanly with git apply (tested)
- Has correct hunk headers
- Ends with proper newline (no trailing blank context lines)
- Can be applied by CI using scripts/apply-quickjs-patches.sh

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# Apply QuickJS patches for frozen interpreter support
+# This script is idempotent - can be run multiple times safely
+
+set -e
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
+QUICKJS_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/quickjs-ng"
+PATCH_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/patches"
+
+echo "=== Applying QuickJS patches ==="
+echo "QuickJS directory: $QUICKJS_DIR"
+
+cd "$QUICKJS_DIR"
+
+# Reset to clean state
+echo "[1/2] Resetting QuickJS to clean state..."
+git reset --hard HEAD
+git clean -fd
+
+# Apply patches
+echo "[2/2] Applying patches..."
+for patch in "$PATCH_DIR"/*.patch; do
+    echo "  Applying: $(basename "$patch")"
+    if git apply "$patch"; then
+        echo "  ✅ Success"
+    else
+        echo "  ❌ Failed to apply patch: $patch"
+        echo "  Note: If patch is corrupt, run scripts/regenerate-quickjs-patch.sh to fix"
+        exit 1
+    fi
+done
+
+echo "✅ All patches applied successfully"
